actions.name: expired
actions.description: Look up all expired tokens.

actions.name: expunge_expired
actions.description: Deletes expired tokens.

actions.name: get_token
actions.description: Look up a token by JTI or token, and an optional purpose.

actions.name: revoke_all_stored_for_subject
actions.description: Revokes all stored tokens for a specific subject.

actions.name: revoke_jti
actions.description: Revoke a token by JTI. Creates a revocation token corresponding to the provided jti.

actions.name: revoke_token
actions.description: Revoke a token. Creates a revocation token corresponding to the provided token.

actions.name: revoked?
actions.description: Returns true if a revocation token is found for the provided token

actions.name: store_token
actions.description: Stores a token used for the provided purpose.

actions.name: get_by_email
actions.description: Looks up a user by their email

actions.name: get_by_subject
actions.description: Get a user by the subject claim in a JWT

actions.name: sign_in_with_magic_link
actions.description: Sign in or register a user with magic link.

arguments.name: token
arguments.description: The token from the magic link that was sent to the user

arguments.name: remember_me
arguments.description: Whether to generate a remember me token

actions.name: sign_in_with_remember_me
actions.description: Attempt to sign in using a remember me token.

arguments.name: token
arguments.description: The remember me token for authenticating

domains.name: Agents
domains.description:
Agent control-plane domain.

Owns agent templates, deployments, runs, messages, and memory.

resources.name: Agent
resources.description:
Agent template definitions.

Stores metadata about available agent templates that can be spawned.

resources.name: AgentDeployment
resources.description:
Operator-managed configured agent deployments.

Deployments are the durable bridge between agent templates and runtime
executions. They hold ownership, visibility, schedule, and configuration.

resources.name: AgentMessage
resources.description:
Conversation history for agent runs.

Stores messages between user and agent, including tool calls and results.

resources.name: AgentRun
resources.description:
Execution tracking for agent runs.

Uses AshStateMachine to track the lifecycle of agent executions:
pending -> running -> completed/failed/cancelled

resources.name: AgentRunOutput
resources.description:
Durable business outputs produced by an `AgentRun`.

These records bridge runtime history to saved business entities like
`ProcurementSource` and `Bid` without forcing the business records themselves to
track a single originating run forever.

resources.name: Memory
resources.description:
Persistent memory storage for agents.

Stores facts, patterns, decisions, and preferences that agents can
remember and recall across sessions.

actions.name: by_key
actions.description: Get a specific memory by its key

actions.name: by_type
actions.description: Find all memories of a specific type

actions.name: recall
actions.description: Search memories by query string matching key or content

actions.name: remember
actions.description: Store a memory with a key and content

actions.name: search
actions.description: Find all memories in a specific namespace

attributes.name: contacts
attributes.description: List of contact info: [{name, title, email, linkedin}]

resources.name: Prospect
resources.description:
Prospect company for sales outreach.

Stores companies discovered through various signals:
- Job postings for controls engineers
- Facility expansions
- Legacy system indicators
- Industry/trade news

actions.name: convert_to_company
actions.description: Link prospect to a company when it converts

actions.name: convert_to_lead
actions.description: Link prospect to a lead for qualification

relationships.name: converted_lead
relationships.description: Lead created when prospect needs qualification

cross_domain_relationships.name: converted_lead
cross_domain_relationships.description: Lead created when prospect needs qualification

domains.name: Procurement
domains.description:
Procurement monitoring and opportunity discovery domain.

Owns monitored procurement sources and the bid opportunities discovered from
them. This is durable business state, not agent control-plane state.

attributes.name: score_geography
attributes.description: 0-20: SoCal = 20, NorCal = 12, Other CA = 8, Out of state = 0

attributes.name: source_url
attributes.description: URL of the lead source that found this

resources.name: Bid
resources.description:
Discovered bid/RFP opportunity.

Stores procurement opportunities found by the BidScanner agent,
including scoring based on service match, geography, value, and tech fit.

actions.name: convert_to_opportunity
actions.description: Link bid to an opportunity when pursuing as a deal

relationships.name: agency_company
relationships.description: Company record for the agency posting the bid

cross_domain_relationships.name: agency_company
cross_domain_relationships.description: Company record for the agency posting the bid

attributes.name: configured_at
attributes.description: When scrape config was saved or source was marked ready

attributes.name: metadata
attributes.description: Additional config: API keys, endpoints, etc.

attributes.name: portal_id
attributes.description: External portal ID (e.g., PlanetBids portal number)

attributes.name: scrape_config
attributes.description: CSS selectors for deterministic scanning (procurement sources)

attributes.name: scrape_selector
attributes.description: CSS selector for bid listings if scraping

resources.name: ProcurementSource
resources.description:
Universal monitored URL.

Every URL we check periodically — whether it's a government procurement
portal, a company website, a job board, or an industry directory. The
`source_type` determines which scanner strategy to use.

## Source Types & Scanners

| Type | Scanner | What it finds |
|------|---------|---------------|
| planetbids, opengov, cal_eprocure, utility, school, port, custom | DeterministicScanner | Bids/RFPs |
| company_site | CompanyScanner | Contacts, hiring signals, news |
| sam_gov | SAM.gov API (future) | Federal opportunities |
| job_board | JobScanner (future) | Hiring signals |
| directory | DirectoryScanner (future) | Company listings |

## State Machine

    found ──→ pending ──→ configured ──→ scanning (loops on cron)
                │              │              │
                ▼              ▼              ▼
           config_failed   scan_failed    (loops)
                │              │
                ▼              ▼
            pending        configured (retry)
                │
                ▼
             manual

Company sites can be created directly in `:configured` state since they
don't need CSS selector discovery.

actions.name: approve
actions.description: Approve this source for configuration and scanning

actions.name: block
actions.description: Block this source from future scanning

actions.name: configure
actions.description: Save discovered scraping configuration

actions.name: create_for_company
actions.description: Create a pre-configured source for a company website

actions.name: ignore
actions.description: Ignore this source without scanning it

actions.name: needs_configuration
actions.description: Sources needing scrape config discovery

actions.name: queue
actions.description: Queue source for SmartScanner configuration

actions.name: ready_for_scan
actions.description: Configured sources due for scanning

actions.name: reconsider
actions.description: Move this source back into the candidate pool

actions.name: scan
actions.description: Run scanner on this source (routed by source_type)

relationships.name: company
relationships.description: Company this source belongs to (for company_site type)

cross_domain_relationships.name: company
cross_domain_relationships.description: Company this source belongs to (for company_site type)

domains.name: Sales
domains.description:
Sales domain for CRM and pipeline management.

Manages companies, contacts, activities, and notes — the relationship
management side of the business. Pipeline resources (Opportunity,
Proposal, Contract) will be added in a future phase.

resources.name: Activity
resources.description:
Activity resource for CRM.

Tracks interactions and touchpoints with companies and contacts —
calls, emails, meetings, site visits, and demos.

relationships.name: bid
relationships.description: Related bid (if activity is about a bid)

relationships.name: lead
relationships.description: Related lead (if activity is about a lead)

relationships.name: opportunity
relationships.description: Related opportunity (if activity is about a deal)

cross_domain_relationships.name: bid
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related bid (if activity is about a bid)

resources.name: Address
resources.description:
Address resource for CRM.

Reusable addresses that can be attached to companies, contacts,
or other entities. Supports multiple address types per company.

resources.name: Company
resources.description:
Company resource for CRM.

Represents organizations — customers, prospects, partners, and vendors.
Central entity that contacts, activities, and notes attach to.

resources.name: CompanyRelationship
resources.description:
Company-to-company relationships.

Tracks associations between companies such as parent/subsidiary,
partner, vendor, or custom relationships. Supports bidirectional
relationships with different types.

relationships.name: from_company
relationships.description: The source company in the relationship

relationships.name: to_company
relationships.description: The target company in the relationship

attributes.name: last_contacted_at
attributes.description: Last time we contacted this person

resources.name: Contact
resources.description:
Contact resource for CRM.

Represents people we interact with during sales and service activities.
Employment history (which companies they work at) is tracked via Employment.

actions.name: by_company
actions.description: Find contacts currently employed at a company

actions.name: decision_makers
actions.description: Find contacts with decision-maker role at current job

actions.name: former_at_company
actions.description: Find contacts who previously worked at a company

actions.name: unemployed
actions.description: Contacts with no current employment

attributes.name: is_primary
attributes.description: Is this contact the primary contact at this company

resources.name: Employment
resources.description:
Employment history for contacts.

Tracks a contact's job history across companies, including
title, department, and tenure. Allows tracking when contacts
move between companies.

actions.name: end_employment
actions.description: Mark employment as ended

attributes.name: metadata
attributes.description: Extra context: score, workflow, source, etc.

attributes.name: subject_id
attributes.description: ID of the record this event is about

attributes.name: subject_type
attributes.description: Type of record: bid, lead, prospect, opportunity

attributes.name: summary
attributes.description: Human-readable one-liner: 'Passed on SCADA bid — not in our area'

resources.name: Event
resources.description:
Append-only decision/audit log.

Records every meaningful state change across the pipeline — passes,
pursues, stage advances, wins, losses — with who did it, why, and
what changed. Never edited or deleted.

Use for:
- Audit trail ("why did we pass on this?")
- Pattern analysis ("what do we keep passing on?")
- Team accountability ("who moved this forward?")

resources.name: Industry
resources.description:
Industry classification for companies.

Simple lookup table with NAICS codes for categorizing customers
by their industry vertical.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: What the company does and why they need our services

attributes.name: source_details
attributes.description: Signal — why this is a lead right now

attributes.name: source_url
attributes.description: URL where we found this lead

resources.name: Lead
resources.description:
Lead resource for CRM.

Pre-qualification workflow for prospects that need vetting
before becoming Companies/Contacts. Can be converted to
Company + Contact + Opportunity when qualified.

actions.name: convert
actions.description: Mark lead as converted after creating Company/Contact/Opportunity

relationships.name: prospect
relationships.description: Source prospect if created from agent discovery

cross_domain_relationships.name: prospect
cross_domain_relationships.description: Source prospect if created from agent discovery

attributes.name: notable_type
attributes.description: Type of parent record: company, contact, activity

resources.name: Note
resources.description:
Polymorphic note resource for CRM.

Freeform notes that can be attached to any CRM record —
companies, contacts, or activities. Uses notable_type + notable_id
pattern for polymorphism.

attributes.name: workflow
attributes.description: Workflow type — determines valid stage progression

resources.name: Opportunity
resources.description:
Opportunity resource for CRM.

Represents sales pipeline opportunities/deals with workflow-driven
stage progression. Three workflows determine valid stage paths:

- `:bid_response` — RFP, RFI, RFQ, SOQ responses
  discovery → review → qualification → drafting → submitted → won/lost

- `:outreach` — cold calls, company approach, prospect outreach
  discovery → research → outreach → meeting → qualification → proposal → negotiation → won/lost

- `:inbound` — referrals, inbound inquiries, trade shows
  discovery → qualification → meeting → proposal → negotiation → won/lost

relationships.name: bid
relationships.description: Source bid if created from a bid

cross_domain_relationships.name: bid
cross_domain_relationships.description: Source bid if created from a bid

attributes.name: context
attributes.description: Why this research is linked to this entity

resources.name: ResearchLink
resources.description:
Join resource linking ResearchRequests to any entity.

Enables a research request to be linked to multiple bids, companies,
opportunities, events, leads, or prospects. Each link carries optional
context explaining the connection.

attributes.name: researchable_id
attributes.description: ID of the entity to research

attributes.name: researchable_type
attributes.description: Type of entity: company, contact, lead, prospect

resources.name: ResearchRequest
resources.description:
Research request for CRM entities.

Tracks research tasks for companies, contacts, leads, and prospects.
Uses AshStateMachine for status workflow.

relationships.name: assigned_to
relationships.description: User assigned to do the research

cross_domain_relationships.name: assigned_to
cross_domain_relationships.description: User assigned to do the research

attributes.name: related_to_type
attributes.description: Type of related record: opportunity, bid, lead

resources.name: Task
resources.description:
Task resource for CRM.

Future to-dos and follow-up tasks related to sales activities.
Can be linked to companies, contacts, opportunities, or other records.
